followupAnswerBank.question: How do you explain the authorization-based company code filter to a project interviewer?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Authorization-based company code filter ensure karta hai ki users sirf un company codes ka data access kar sakein jinhe view karne ke liye authorized hain. ABAP mein, main T001 se saare company codes loop karta hoon aur har ek ke liye AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT F_BKPF_BUK ID BUKRS run karta hoon. Main authorized codes ko ek RANGES table mein collect karta hoon. WHERE clause mein, main AND conditions use karke user-entered s_bukrs range aur authorization range dono apply karta hoon. Additionally, main ek custom F4 help provide karta hoon jo sirf authorized company codes dikhata hai, taaki users ko start se valid selections ke liye guide kiya jaaye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The authorization-based company code filter ensures users can only access data for company codes they are authorized to view. In ABAP, I loop through all company codes from T001 and run AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT F_BKPF_BUK ID BUKRS for each one. I collect the authorized codes into a RANGES table. In the WHERE clause, I apply both the user-entered s_bukrs range and the authorization range using AND conditions. Additionally, I provide a custom F4 help that shows only authorized company codes, so users are guided to valid selections from the start.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Multi-company financial report mein global admin sab 47 companies dekh sakta tha. Regional manager sirf apne 5 companies. AUTHORITY-CHECK se dynamically authorized codes filter kiye. Custom F4 bhi authorized codes hi dikhata tha. Audit ke waqt compliance officer ne confirm kiya — koi unauthorized data access nahi tha.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf F4 filter karna aur WHERE clause mein authorization check nahi karna — savvy user manually company code type kar sakta hai F4 bypass karke. Both UI (F4) aur backend (WHERE clause) pe restriction zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
RANGES: r_auth_bukrs FOR t001-bukrs.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  " Build authorization-filtered range
  SELECT bukrs FROM t001 INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_all).
  LOOP AT lt_all INTO DATA(ls_bukrs).
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
      ID 'BUKRS' FIELD ls_bukrs-bukrs
      ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      r_auth_bukrs-sign   = 'I'.
      r_auth_bukrs-option = 'EQ'.
      r_auth_bukrs-low    = ls_bukrs-bukrs.
      APPEND r_auth_bukrs.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Apply both user filter AND authorization filter
  SELECT * FROM bkpf INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs)
    WHERE bukrs IN @s_bukrs
      AND bukrs IN @r_auth_bukrs.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the project scenario for radio button-based mode switching on a selection screen?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek common real-time scenario selection screen pe radio buttons dwara controlled ek dual-mode report hai. Management sirf totals ke saath summary chahta hai; operational team additional filter fields ke saath line-by-line detail chahti hai. Radio button group control karta hai kaunsa mode active hai. AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein, MODIF ID additional filter fields show ya hide karne ke liye use hota hai — sirf Detail mode mein visible. START-OF-SELECTION mein, report logic radio button value pe branch karta hai ya toh data aggregate karne ke liye ya full detail lines fetch karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A common real-time scenario is a dual-mode report controlled by radio buttons on the selection screen. Management wants a summary with totals only; the operational team wants line-by-line detail with additional filter fields. The radio button group controls which mode is active. In AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT, MODIF ID is used to show or hide the additional filter fields — visible only in Detail mode. In START-OF-SELECTION, the report logic branches on the radio button value to either aggregate data or fetch full detail lines.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Inventory report banaya: Summary mode mein plant-level stock totals, Detail mode mein individual storage location and batch details. Jab Detail mode select hota tha, storage location filter field appear hoti thi via MODIF ID 'DT'. Summary mode mein woh hidden rehti thi — simpler screen. Same report, two audiences.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Radio button change karne pe screen refresh nahi karna — MODIF ID changes AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein hi apply hote hain jo screen redraw pe trigger hoti hai. Radio button change se automatic redraw nahi hota — user koi aur action kare tabhi dikhe. This is expected SAP behavior, not a bug.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
PARAMETERS: p_sum RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1 DEFAULT 'X',
            p_det RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1.

" Fields visible only in Detail mode
PARAMETERS: p_lgort TYPE lgort_d MODIF ID 'DT',
            p_charg TYPE charg_d MODIF ID 'DT'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-group1 = 'DT'.
      screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_det = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
      MODIFY SCREEN.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  IF p_sum = 'X'.
    " Summary: aggregate totals
    SELECT werks SUM( labst ) AS total_stock
      FROM mard INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_summary)
      GROUP BY werks.
  ELSE.
    " Detail: line-level with extra filters
    SELECT werks lgort charg labst FROM mard
      INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_detail)
      WHERE lgort = @p_lgort.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you describe your F4 help development experience in a project interview?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Mere project mein, maine material group field ke liye ek dependent custom F4 help implement kiya. Business requirement thi ki material group popup sirf un material groups ko display kare jinme user dwara already selected plant mein active materials ho. AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_matkl mein, main check karta hoon ki p_werks filled hai ya nahi — agar nahi, toh main user ko pehle plant select karne ke liye keh kar ek information message display karta hoon. Agar p_werks filled hai, main MARA se ek SELECT DISTINCT matkl plant se filtered execute karta hoon aur ise value_org S ke saath F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST ko pass karta hoon. Main debugging challenge ek initial DYNPROFIELD mismatch tha jisne popup ko open karwaya lekin field populate nahi kiya — case fix karne se yeh resolve ho gaya.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In my project, I implemented a dependent custom F4 help for the material group field. The business requirement was that the material group popup should only display material groups that have active materials in the plant the user already selected. In AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_matkl, I check if p_werks is filled — if not, I display an information message asking the user to select a plant first. If p_werks is filled, I execute a SELECT DISTINCT matkl from MARA filtered by plant and pass it to F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST with value_org S. The main debugging challenge was an initial DYNPROFIELD mismatch that caused the popup to open but not populate the field — fixing the case resolved it.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Procurement report real-time challenge: Plant list 300+ plants thi. F4 popup open hone mein 8 seconds lag rahe the full T001W load ke saath. Optimize kiya: pehle SELECT kiya sirf plants where materials exist in that plant — result 47 plants. F4 popup instantly opens. Performance optimization as part of F4 development.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: F4 implement karne ke baad return value assign karna bhool jaana — lt_returns read karna aur p_matkl = ls_return-fieldval karna zaroori hai. Without explicit assignment field empty rehti hai even after user selection.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_matkl.
  " Graceful check for dependent parent field
  IF p_werks IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Select plant first before using material group F4' TYPE 'I'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_matkl,
           matkl TYPE matkl,
           wgbez TYPE wgbez,
         END OF ty_matkl.
  DATA: lt_matkl  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_matkl,
        lt_return TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddshretval,
        ls_return TYPE ddshretval.

  SELECT DISTINCT m~matkl t~wgbez
    FROM mara AS m JOIN t023t AS t ON m~matkl = t~matkl
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @lt_matkl
    WHERE m~werks = @p_werks AND t~spras = @sy-langu.

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING retfield = 'MATKL' dynpprog = sy-repid
      dynpnr = sy-dynnr dynprofield = 'P_MATKL' value_org = 'S'
    TABLES value_tab = lt_matkl return_tab = lt_return.

  IF lt_return IS NOT INITIAL.
    READ TABLE lt_return INDEX 1 INTO ls_return.
    p_matkl = ls_return-fieldval.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is a real example of using OBLIGATORY and AT SELECTION-SCREEN validation together?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek real project mein, maine company code aur fiscal year fields ke liye OBLIGATORY use kiya enforce karne ke liye ki execution se pehle yeh fill hon. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein, maine further validations add kiye: check karna ki p_bukrs T001 mein exist karta hai typos catch karne ke liye, AUTHORITY-CHECK use karke verify karna ki user ko entered company code ke liye authorization hai, aur validate karna ki s_datum-low s_datum-high se greater nahi hai. Yeh do mechanisms ek saath kaam karte hain — OBLIGATORY empty submission prevent karta hai, aur AT SELECTION-SCREEN entered values ki correctness aur business rules validate karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In a real project, I used OBLIGATORY for the company code and fiscal year fields to enforce they are filled before execution. In AT SELECTION-SCREEN, I added further validations: checking that p_bukrs exists in T001 to catch typos, verifying the user has authorization for the entered company code using AUTHORITY-CHECK, and validating that s_datum-low is not greater than s_datum-high. These two mechanisms work together — OBLIGATORY prevents empty submission, and AT SELECTION-SCREEN validates the correctness and business rules of the entered values.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Vendor payment report: OBLIGATORY pe company code, fiscal year. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein: company code T001 mein exist karta hai, user authorized hai, date range valid hai, fiscal year range sensible hai (not more than 2 years span — business rule). Multi-layer validation ne production support tickets 70% reduce kiye.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: OBLIGATORY ke baad AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein duplicate empty check likhna — redundant code. OBLIGATORY already kaam karta hai PARAMETERS ke liye. AT SELECTION-SCREEN focus karo existence aur business rule validations pe.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY,   " Layer 1: must be filled
            p_gjahr TYPE gjahr OBLIGATORY.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.  " Layer 2: correctness validation
  " Check company code exists
  SELECT SINGLE bukrs FROM t001 INTO @DATA(lv_check)
    WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |Company code { p_bukrs } not found in system| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  " Authorization check
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |Not authorized for company code { p_bukrs }| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  " Business rule: date range not more than 1 year
  IF s_datum-low IS NOT INITIAL AND s_datum-high IS NOT INITIAL.
    IF ( s_datum-high - s_datum-low ) > 365.
      MESSAGE 'Date range cannot exceed 365 days' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is a real project challenge you faced with MODIF ID and how did you solve it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: MODIF ID ke saath ek real challenge yeh thi ki jab user Summary aur Detail mode ke beech switch karne ke liye ek radio button click karta tha, dependent fields immediately show ya hide nahi hote the — screen static appear hoti thi jab tak user Enter press nahi karta ya doosre field pe move nahi karta. Yeh isliye hai kyunki AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT sirf ek screen action se triggered PBO cycle ke dauran fire hota hai. Maine yeh behavior document kiya aur functional team ko advise kiya ki radio button change karne ke baad Enter press karna mode switch activate karta hai. Ek alternative dynamic screen building CALL SELECTION-SCREEN se use karna hota lekin yeh complexity introduce karta jo humne add nahi karna choose kiya.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A real challenge I faced with MODIF ID was that when the user clicked a radio button to switch between Summary and Detail mode, the dependent fields did not immediately show or hide — the screen appeared static until the user pressed Enter or moved to another field. This is because AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT fires only during a PBO cycle triggered by a screen action. I documented this behavior and advised the functional team that pressing Enter after changing the radio button activates the mode switch. An alternative would be using dynamic screen building with CALL SELECTION-SCREEN but that introduced complexity we chose not to add.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Business user complaint: 'Radio button select kiya but extra fields nahi dikhe.' Investigated: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT sahi kaam kar raha tha — MODIF ID logic correct tha. Issue sirf yeh tha ki screen refresh tabhi hoti hai jab user Enter dabata hai. Solution: Selection screen mein ek small text added: 'Press Enter after changing mode.' Simple documentation fix resolved user confusion.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: MODIF ID logic AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein (validation event mein) likhne ki koshish karna — MODIF ID changes sirf AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT (PBO event) mein kaam karte hain. Wrong event mein likha gaya MODIF ID code silently ignored hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
PARAMETERS: p_sum RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1 DEFAULT 'X',
            p_det RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1.
PARAMETERS: p_lgort TYPE lgort_d MODIF ID 'DT'.

" CORRECT: MODIF ID logic in OUTPUT event (PBO)
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-group1 = 'DT'.
      " Fires on each screen render including after Enter
      screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_det = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
      MODIFY SCREEN.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

" WRONG: MODIF ID logic in validation event (does NOT work)
" AT SELECTION-SCREEN.  " This event is PAI - too late for screen changes
"   LOOP AT SCREEN.
"     IF screen-group1 = 'DT'.
"       screen-active = 0.  " SILENTLY IGNORED here
"       MODIFY SCREEN.
"     ENDIF.
"   ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you handle background job scheduling for a selection screen report in your project?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Background scheduling ke liye, maine report ke INITIALIZATION event ko design kiya dynamic defaults set karne ke liye — sy-datum se computed last month ki dates, default company code ke roop mein current client. Maine PROD_MONTH_END named ek production variant create kiya saare OBLIGATORY fields aur important optional fields cover karte hue. SM36 mein, maine report name aur is variant assigned ke saath ek periodic job create kiya. Maine ise verify kiya pehle Immediate start ke saath SM36 mein immediately run karke, phir job log aur output ke liye SM37 check kiya. Maine audit trail purposes ke liye execution log ko ek Z-table mein write karne ke liye START-OF-SELECTION mein sy-batch = 'X' checks bhi add kiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For background scheduling, I designed the report's INITIALIZATION event to set dynamic defaults — last month's dates computed from sy-datum, current client as default company code. I created a production variant named PROD_MONTH_END covering all OBLIGATORY fields and important optional fields. In SM36, I created a periodic job with the report name and this variant assigned. I verified by running it immediately in SM36 with Immediate start, then checked SM37 for the job log and output. I also added sy-batch = 'X' checks in START-OF-SELECTION to write an execution log to a Z-table for audit trail purposes.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Month-end aging report: Variant AGING_MONTH_END banaya. INITIALIZATION mein date range automatically compute hota tha — previous month first day to last day, sy-datum se calculate. Production job 1 tarikh ko midnight chalti thi. SM37 monitoring ke liye BASIS team ko variant aur schedule details di. Zero manual intervention required every month.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Background variant mein OBLIGATORY field empty chhodna — job run hogi, immediately fail hogi OBLIGATORY error se. Aur user-specific variant (slash prefix) background job mein assign karna — agar woh user deactivate ho toh job fail.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zbackground_ready.

PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.

INITIALIZATION.
  " Dynamic dates - work correctly in any variant context
  DATA(lv_first_prev) = |{ sy-datum(4) }{ sy-datum+4(2) }01|.
  s_datum-sign   = 'I'.
  s_datum-option = 'BT'.
  s_datum-low    = lv_first_prev - 100.  " Approximate prev month
  s_datum-high   = lv_first_prev - 1.   " Last day of prev month
  APPEND s_datum.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  IF sy-batch = 'X'.
    " Background-specific: write execution log
    INSERT INTO zexec_log VALUES @(
      VALUE #( repid = sy-repid
               datum = sy-datum
               uzeit = sy-uzeit
               bukrs = p_bukrs ) ).
  ENDIF.
  " Main report logic follows...

followupAnswerBank.question: What did you learn about selection screen performance from real project experience?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Real project work se ek key performance lesson: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT har screen interaction pe fire hota hai — har Enter key press, har F4, har field change. Agar tum is event ke andar database queries run karte ho, yeh repeatedly execute hote hain. Ek project mein, mere paas AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT ke andar ek large Z-configuration table pe SELECT tha jo har screen render pe chalta tha. Slow network pe users har field change pe 2-3 second delays notice karte the. Fix: SELECT ko INITIALIZATION mein move kiya ek STATICS variable ko ek cache flag ke roop mein use karke — data ek baar load, saare subsequent output event calls pe used.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A key performance lesson from real project work: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT fires on every screen interaction — every Enter key press, every F4, every field change. If you run database queries inside this event, they execute repeatedly. In one project, I had a SELECT on a large Z-configuration table inside AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT that ran on every screen render. Users on slow network noticed 2-3 second delays on each field change. Fix: moved the SELECT to INITIALIZATION using a STATICS variable as a cache flag — data loaded once, used on all subsequent output event calls.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Konkret numbers: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein tha ek SELECT from ZTABLE (10,000 rows) for building authorized company codes list. User observed 3-second lag on each field entry. Profiled with SAT (ABAP runtime analysis) — confirmed bottleneck. Moved to INITIALIZATION with static cache. Output event lag: 3 seconds → 0.1 seconds. User complaint resolved.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT pe COMMIT WORK ya ROLLBACK WORK calling karna — yeh events dialog work process mein hote hain, database commits yahan appropriate nahi hain. Data modifications selection screen events mein avoid karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zperformance_aware.

PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.

" Cache variable to avoid repeated SELECT
DATA: gt_auth_bukrs TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF t001,
      gv_loaded     TYPE abap_bool.

INITIALIZATION.
  " Load ONCE at program start
  SELECT bukrs butxt FROM t001 INTO TABLE @gt_auth_bukrs.
  gv_loaded = abap_true.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  " NO SELECT here - use cached data
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-name = 'P_BUKRS'.
      " Use gt_auth_bukrs (already loaded) for validation display
      " No database call!
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.
  " If NOT using this pattern, each Enter press = new SELECT = slow!

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you document selection screen design for handover to another developer?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Handover documentation ke liye, maine inline ABAP comments maintain kiye explain karte hue har selection screen parameter ka purpose, uska default source, koi MODIF ID jisme yeh belong karta hai, aur F4 help source. AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT se pehle ek brief block comment MODIF ID grouping logic aur kaunsa radio button kaunse group ko control karta hai explain karta hai. Maine ek simple one-page design document bhi create kiya saare parameters, unke types, mandatory flag, validation rules, aur known production variants unke purpose ke saath list karte hue. Yeh transport request number ke saath alongside project wiki mein stored tha.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For handover documentation, I maintained inline ABAP comments explaining each selection screen parameter's purpose, its default source, any MODIF ID it belongs to, and the F4 help source. A brief block comment before AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT explains the MODIF ID grouping logic and which radio button controls which group. I also created a simple one-page design document listing all parameters, their types, mandatory flag, validation rules, and known production variants with their purpose. This was stored in the project wiki alongside the transport request number.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ek report mein 12 fields the — complex MODIF ID logic. Developer changed project 6 months baad. New developer ne code padha — comments clear nahi the, 2 days debugging mein gaye. Lesson learned: Retrofit kiya detailed comments throughout code. Next new developer onboarded in 2 hours instead of 2 days.

selection-screens-026.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Production incident ka matlab hai — real users production system mein kaam kar rahe hain aur suddenly kuch kaam karna band kar diya. Selection screen pe incident ka example: Koi field suddenly gayab ho gaya, ya F4 help kaam nahi kar raha, ya validation error aa raha hai joh pehle nahi aata tha. Yeh ek serious situation hai kyunki business ruka hua hota hai. Developer ka kaam hai quickly investigate karo, root cause dhundo, aur fix karo — ideally bina production downtime ke. Selection screen incidents mostly in causes hote hain: Kisi ne latest transport liya jisme screen logic change tha. AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein MODIF ID condition galat ho gayi. Kisi authorization change se field access change hua. Kisi ne accidentally MODIF ID condition zyada broad ya narrow kar di. Real life mein: Subah 8 baje call aata hai — 'Report ke saab company code field dikhai nahi de raha!' Developer investigates: transport log dekha — kal raat ek transport aaya tha jisme AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein condition change thi. Revert kiya — field wapas aayi. Permanent fix: condition sahi kiya aur test kiya.
selection-screens-026.hinglishMasterExplanation: Production incident handling ke liye structured approach samjho: Step 1: Immediate triage. Kya exact problem hai? Kaunsi field missing hai? Error message kya hai? Kab se ho raha hai? Kaunse users affect hain? Production system accessible hai? Transaction code kya hai report ke liye? Yeh information pehle collect karo caller se. Step 2: Check recent transports. STMS ya SE09/SE10 se dekho kya recent transports aaye hain. Selection screen se related programs — ZREPORT_NAME — ke liye recent imports dekho. Transport log mein kya change tha? Compare karo current code vs previous version. Version comparison: SE38 mein program open karo, Version management se previous version se compare karo. Step 3: Common root causes check. AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein MODIF ID condition — kya condition logic badal gayi? LOOP AT SCREEN mein screen-name ya screen-group1 filter — kya wrong field name aa gayi? Authorization object change — kya kisi ne authorization check tighten kiya? Reference field change — agar SELECT-OPTIONS FOR clause mein field change tha? Step 4: Fix strategy. Agar transport revert possible hai — transport ko regress karo (safer for quick fix). Agar sirf code fix chahiye — emergency transport banao, test karo QA mein (if time), production import karo. Agar workaround possible hai — functional team ko alternative batao (different transaction, manual entry). Step 5: Post-incident. Root cause document karo. Same issue ke prevention ke liye code review aur transport change description policy enforce karo. Monitoring add karo agar possible. SELECTION SCREEN SPECIFIC INCIDENTS: Incident Type 1: Field not visible. Check AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein screen-active = 0 wrongly set. Check MODIF ID condition — kya condition always false evaluate ho rahi hai? Incident Type 2: F4 help not working. Check AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR event — kya event itself missing hai ya FM call fail ho rahi hai? Check sy-subrc after FM call — exception parameter_error. Incident Type 3: Unexpected validation error. Check AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein condition change — kya validation logic tighten hua jo pehle valid values accept karta tha?
selection-screens-026.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'When a production incident occurs for a selection screen — like a field suddenly becoming invisible or an F4 help stopping to work — my first step is to collect the exact symptoms from the user: which field, which transaction, since when, and any error messages. Next I check the recent transport history using STMS to see if any transports were imported to production recently that touch the affected program. Most selection screen incidents I have seen are caused by AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT logic where a MODIF ID condition was changed in a recent transport, accidentally setting screen-active to zero for the wrong field. I compare the current version of the program with the previous version using SE38 Version Management. Once I identify the cause, I either revert the transport if safe, or create an emergency fix transport after verifying in QA. I also check if an authorization change could have triggered the issue — for example, if a MODIF ID condition was using AUTHORITY-CHECK result to hide a field and the auth object was modified.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein production incident answer mein hamesha structured approach do: Triage → Transport check → Code comparison → Root cause → Fix → Prevention. Yeh shows maturity aur real-world problem solving. Sirf 'main debug karta' bolna sufficient nahi hai — process bolna zaroori hai.
selection-screens-026.codeExamples:
REPORT zinvestigate_incident.

" Typical incident: field not visible on production
" Root cause found: MODIF ID condition accidentally too broad

PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY,
            p_werks TYPE werks_d MODIF ID 'PL'.

PARAMETERS: p_sum RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1 DEFAULT 'X',
            p_det RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1.

" BUGGY VERSION (after wrong transport):
" AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
"   LOOP AT SCREEN.
"     IF screen-group1 = 'PL' OR screen-name = 'P_BUKRS'.
"       screen-active = 0.  " BUG: p_bukrs also hidden!
"     ENDIF.
"     MODIFY SCREEN.
"   ENDLOOP.

" CORRECT VERSION:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-group1 = 'PL'.   " Only MODIF ID group PL
      screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_det = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.  " ALWAYS call MODIFY SCREEN in loop
  ENDLOOP.

" Check version management in SE38 to compare:
" Utilities -> Version Management -> Display differences
" This is how you find what changed in the transport

selection-screens-026.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Production-incident handling for selection screens — disappeared fields, broken F4, unexpected validation errors after a transport.

Used for:
- Triaging symptoms (which field, which T-code, since when, recent transports).
- Comparing current vs previous code in SE38 Version Management.
- Reverting the bad transport or shipping an emergency fix with peer review.

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT + LOOP AT SCREEN + screen-group1 + MODIFY SCREEN.

followupAnswerBank.question: What are the first three things you check when a selection screen field is suddenly not visible in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Achanak invisible selection screen field ke liye first three checks hain: ek, STMS ya SE10 ke through recent transports review karo identify karne ke liye agar koi program change imported hua jo AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT logic ko touch karta ho. Do, affected program ke liye AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT block ko SE38 mein directly examine karo — MODIF ID conditions dekho jo wrong field ya group ke liye screen-active ko zero set kar rahe ho. Teen, SE38 Version Management use karke current program version ko previous se compare karo — diff view exactly highlight karta hai kaunsi lines change hui hain, bug ko immediately visible banata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The first three checks for a suddenly invisible selection screen field are: one, review recent transports via STMS or SE10 to identify if any program change was imported that touches AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT logic. Two, examine the AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT block directly in SE38 for the affected program — look for MODIF ID conditions that may be incorrectly setting screen-active to zero for the wrong field or group. Three, use SE38 Version Management to compare the current program version with the previous one — the diff view highlights exactly what lines changed, making the bug immediately visible.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Monday morning incident: plant field gone. Checked STMS — Friday evening ek transport. SE38 version compare — 3 lines changed in AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT. Bug: OR condition accidentally included 'PL' group when it should have been excluded from hide logic. 15 minutes root cause identified. Emergency transport with fix — 45 minutes total resolution time.

" Investigation process in code:
" Step 1: Check which transports touch this program

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use SE38 Version Management to investigate a selection screen incident?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE38 mein, affected program open karo aur Utilities then Version Management pe navigate karo. Program versions ki ek list timestamps aur transport request numbers ke saath appear hoti hai. Suspected transport se theek pehle ki version select karo aur Compare function use karke current version se compare karo. System ek side-by-side diff display karta hai jisme removed lines red mein marked hain aur added lines green mein. AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT section aur AT SELECTION-SCREEN validation section pe focus karo condition changes find karne ke liye jo observed behavior cause kar sake.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In SE38, open the affected program and navigate to Utilities then Version Management. A list of program versions appears with timestamps and transport request numbers. Select the version just before the suspected transport and compare it with the current version using the Compare function. The system displays a side-by-side diff with removed lines marked in red and added lines in green. Focus on the AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT section and AT SELECTION-SCREEN validation section to find condition changes that could cause the observed behavior.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Version Management diff mein exactly ek line changed thi: screen-active = 0 condition mein additional OR clause add hua tha. Without version comparison, manually reading 200 lines of code mein yeh ek line miss ho sakti thi. Version Management ne incident time 4 hours se 15 minutes kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Version Management timestamps ko transport import timestamps se match nahi karna — production transport import time alag ho sakta hai from when the transport was released in development. Use transport request number (not just time) for accurate matching.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE38 Version Management navigation (process, not code):
" 1. SE38 → Enter program name → Display
" 2. Menu: Utilities → Versions → Version Management
" 3. List shows: Version | Date | Time | Transport | User
" 4. Select OLD version (before suspect transport)
" 5. Select CURRENT version
" 6. Click 'Compare Versions' button
" 7. Diff shown - look for AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT changes

" What you might see in the diff - the bug:
" OLD (green - was correct):
"   IF screen-group1 = 'DT'.

" CURRENT (red - changed, broken):
"   IF screen-group1 = 'DT' OR screen-group1 = 'CO'.
"   " 'CO' group fields now also being hidden - BUG!

" Fix: revert the OR condition
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-group1 = 'DT'.  " Only DT group
      screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_det = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the process for creating an emergency fix transport for a selection screen production incident?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Emergency fix transport process: Pehle, development mein issue reproduce karo root cause confirm karne ke liye. Development system mein SE38 mein code fix apply karo. Specifically is emergency fix ke liye ek naya Workbench transport request create karo — TR description mein ITSM/ServiceNow incident ticket number note karo. Agar QA environment available hai, wahan import aur test karo 5-10 minutes ke liye. Basis team support ke saath, STMS ke through production mein import karo. Production mein fix verify karo. Change management tool mein root cause, impact duration, fix details, aur prevention steps ke saath incident document karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Emergency fix transport process: First, reproduce the issue in development to confirm the root cause. Apply the code fix in SE38 in development system. Create a new Workbench transport request specifically for this emergency fix — note the ITSM/ServiceNow incident ticket number in the TR description. If QA environment is available, import and test there for 5-10 minutes. With basis team support, import to production via STMS. Verify the fix in production. Document the incident in the change management tool with root cause, impact duration, fix details, and prevention steps.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production fix in 45 minutes: 10 min — root cause identified via version comparison. 5 min — code fix in development. 5 min — transport created and released. 10 min — QA import and quick test. 15 min — production import with BASIS team help, user confirmed field visible again. Post-incident root cause document sent to team same day.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Emergency transport mein multiple unrelated changes include karna — emergency fix sirf minimum necessary change hona chahiye. Multiple changes in emergency TR = risk of introducing new bugs.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Emergency fix checklist (process documentation):
" 1. Document: Incident ticket #INC0012345
" 2. Root cause: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT MODIF ID condition
"    changed in TR DEV-K900123, imported Fri 22:00
" 3. Fix: Revert condition to original
" 4. Transport: DEVK900456 (emergency fix TR)

" The actual fix in code:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    " BEFORE BUG (wrong - hid company code too):
    " IF screen-group1 = 'DT' OR screen-name = 'P_BUKRS'.
    "   screen-active = 0.

    " AFTER FIX (correct - only hide DT group):
    IF screen-group1 = 'DT'.
      screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_det = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.
" TR DEVK900456 released, imported to QAS then PRD

followupAnswerBank.question: How does an authorization change cause a selection screen incident?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Authorization changes selection screen incidents do tariko se cause kar sakte hain. Pehla, agar AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein MODIF ID visibility logic AUTHORITY-CHECK use karta hai decide karne ke liye ki field visible hona chahiye ya nahi, aur phir authorization object assignment ek user role ke liye change ho jaata hai, field visibility accordingly change ho jaati hai — affected users ke liye appear ya disappear karte hue. Doosra, agar AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein ek explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK hai jo pehle pass ho raha tha lekin ab fail ho raha hai kyunki authorization object user ke role se remove ho gaya, users achanak error messages get karte hain jo pehle kabhi nahi dekhe the. Investigation ke liye recent ABAP transports AND recent authorization role transports dono check karna padta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Authorization changes can cause selection screen incidents in two ways. First, if the MODIF ID visibility logic in AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT uses AUTHORITY-CHECK to decide whether a field should be visible, and then the authorization object assignment changes for a user role, the field visibility changes accordingly — appearing or disappearing for affected users. Second, if AT SELECTION-SCREEN contains an explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK that was passing before but now fails because the authorization object was removed from the user's role, users suddenly get error messages they never saw before. Investigating requires checking both recent ABAP transports AND recent authorization role transports.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Incident: Finance analyst reported that company code field shows error 'Not authorized' since Monday. ABAP code unchanged — no recent ABAP transports. Found: Basis team changed authorization role SAP_FI_ANALYST on Monday — accidentally removed F_BKPF_BUK authorization object. Authorization admin added object back — incident closed. ABAP developer's role: identify the AUTHORITY-CHECK in code, communicate to Basis what auth object is needed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf ABAP transport log check karna authorization incidents mein — role/profile transports alag system mein import hote hain aur ABAP transport log mein nahi dikhte. Authorization-related incidents mein always BASIS team se role change history check karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Code with AUTHORITY-CHECK in selection screen
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " This check could cause incident if auth object changes
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    " This error appears if auth removed from user's role
    MESSAGE |Not authorized for company { p_bukrs }| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

" Incident investigation: check auth object in SU53
" SU53 shows last failed authorization check for a user
" Useful for identifying which auth object is missing

" Also check MODIF ID visibility based on auth:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-name = 'P_ADMIN'.
      AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'Z_ADMIN' ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.
      screen-active = COND #( WHEN sy-subrc = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
      MODIFY SCREEN.  " Field visible only for admin users
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you communicate a production selection screen incident to stakeholders?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek selection screen production incident ke liye, main ek structured communication approach follow karta hoon. Ownership lene ke 5 minutes ke andar, main reporting user aur unke manager ko ek acknowledgment bhejta hoon: issue known hai, main investigate kar raha hoon. Investigation ke dauran 15-30 minute intervals pe, main status updates provide karta hoon yeh batate hue ki kya ruled out hua hai aur kya check ho raha hai. Root cause identify hone ke baad, main ise non-technical terms mein clearly communicate karta hoon: 'Ek recent system update ne ek filter field ke liye display logic change ki, jo hidden ho gaya.' Fix deploy hone ke baad, main time, ek brief non-technical explanation, aur koi user action required jaise browser cache clear karna ya transaction re-open karna ke saath ek resolution notification bhejta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For a selection screen production incident, I follow a structured communication approach. Within 5 minutes of taking ownership, I send an acknowledgment to the reporting user and their manager: the issue is known, I am investigating. At 15-30 minute intervals during investigation, I provide status updates with what has been ruled out and what is being checked. Once root cause is identified, I communicate it clearly in non-technical terms: 'A recent system update changed the display logic for a filter field, which caused it to become hidden.' After the fix is deployed, I send a resolution notification with the time, a brief non-technical explanation, and any user action required — such as clearing browser cache or re-opening the transaction.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Finance director ko 9 AM incident report kiya. 9:05: 'Ack sent — investigating.' 9:20: 'Root cause found — recent system update. Fix in progress.' 9:45: 'Fixed. Company code field now visible. Please refresh transaction.' Director replied: 'Thanks for quick resolution and clear updates.' Clear communication prevented escalation to CIO level.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Technical jargon stakeholders ko bolna — 'MODIF ID condition mein bug tha AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein' — business user ko yeh samajh nahi aata. Simple language: 'A logic error in the screen display code hid the field.' Communication audience ke hisaab se adjust karo.

followupAnswerBank.question: What preventive measures can you put in place to reduce selection screen production incidents?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Selection screen incidents ke liye key preventive measures hain: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT touch karne wale changes ke liye especially mandatory detailed transport descriptions; MODIF ID logic changes ke liye specifically peer code review kyunki yeh high-impact aur error-prone hain; selection screen reports ke liye ek dedicated QA test checklist saare MODIF ID toggle states, F4 help scenarios, aur background variant execution cover karte hue; production imports ko business hours tak limit karne wali transport scheduling policy prior notification ke saath; aur jahan ECATT available hai, critical selection screens ke liye automated regression scripts jo har transport import ke baad chalte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Key preventive measures for selection screen incidents are: mandatory detailed transport descriptions especially for changes touching AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT; peer code review specifically for MODIF ID logic changes since these are high-impact and error-prone; a dedicated QA test checklist for selection screen reports covering all MODIF ID toggle states, F4 help scenarios, and background variant execution; transport scheduling policy limiting production imports to business hours with prior notification; and where ECATT is available, automated regression scripts for critical selection screens that run after each transport import.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post-incident prevention at a client: Created mandatory transport description template for selection screen changes. Implemented peer review rule — any AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT change needs second developer review. QA checklist added to test documentation. Result: Zero selection screen production incidents in 8 months after implementation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Prevention overkill — har selection screen change ke liye 2-week testing cycle mandatory karna — realistic nahi, team start ignore karne lagti hai. Prevention measures practical aur proportional honi chahiye risk ke hisaab se.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Prevention: Defensive MODIF ID coding pattern
" Always be explicit - avoid ambiguous conditions

" BAD - ambiguous, easy to introduce bugs:
" IF screen-group1 <> 'AB'.  " Double negative - confusing
"   screen-active = 0.

" GOOD - explicit and clear:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    CASE screen-group1.
      WHEN 'DT'.  " Date detail fields
        screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_det = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
      WHEN 'PL'.  " Plant-specific fields
        screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_werks IS NOT INITIAL THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
      WHEN OTHERS.  " All other fields: always visible
        screen-active = 1.
    ENDCASE.
    MODIFY SCREEN.  " Must always be called inside loop
  ENDLOOP.
" CASE statement is self-documenting - each group has clear rule
" New developer can understand without debugging

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a production incident where F4 help is suddenly returning wrong values?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Wrong values return karne wali F4 help ke liye, investigation AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR event block pe focus karta hai. Recent transports check karo is event mein changes ke liye. Version compare karke dekho ki filtering logic — typically internal table filter karne wala ek WHERE clause — change hua tha. Yeh bhi check karo ki F4 function module call mein proper exception handling hai: agar F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST parameter_error throw karta hai aur exception caught nahi hai, system unfiltered values dikhane wale ek default search help pe fall back ho sakta hai. Verify karo ki RETFIELD aur DYNPROFIELD parameters transport ke baad bhi correct hain. Agar ek configuration table F4 filter drive karta hai, check karo ki us config table mein recent data changes unexpected values cause kar rahe ho.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For an F4 help returning wrong values, investigation focuses on the AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR event block. Check recent transports for changes to this event. Version compare to see if the filtering logic — typically a WHERE clause filtering the internal table — was changed. Also check if the F4 function module call has proper exception handling: if F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST throws a parameter_error and the exception is not caught, the system may fall back to a default search help showing unfiltered values. Verify the RETFIELD and DYNPROFIELD parameters are still correct after the transport. If a configuration table drives the F4 filter, check if recent data changes in that config table caused the unexpected values.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production: Procurement analyst called — plant F4 showing all 300 plants instead of authorized 12. Version compare showed: WHERE clause in F4 event had authorization filter removed in last transport (junior developer removed it thinking it was unused code). Restored the WHERE clause filter. Emergency transport. 35 minutes total.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: F4 incident mein sirf ABAP code check karna — agar F4 config table (Z-table) se values aati hain toh table data bhi check karo. Table data change ho sakta hai bina ABAP transport ke (direct table entry via SM30).
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" F4 incident-prone code - no exception handling:
" AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_werks.
"   SELECT werks FROM t001w INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_plants).
"   CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
"     ... EXCEPTIONS NOT HANDLED ...  " Risk: silent fallback

" Incident-safe F4 implementation:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_werks.
  DATA: lt_plants  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF t001w,
        lt_returns TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddshretval.

  " Authorization-filtered plant list - DO NOT REMOVE this WHERE
  SELECT werks name1 FROM t001w
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @lt_plants
    WHERE werks IN @r_auth_werks.   " auth filter - critical!

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING retfield = 'WERKS' dynpprog = sy-repid
      dynpnr = sy-dynnr dynprofield = 'P_WERKS' value_org = 'S'
    TABLES value_tab = lt_plants return_tab = lt_returns
    EXCEPTIONS
      parameter_error = 1   " Handle exception explicitly
      no_values_found = 2.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'F4 help error - contact support' TYPE 'I'.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is a post-incident review for a selection screen issue and what does it cover?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek post-incident review issue introduce hone se le kar resolve hone tak ki complete timeline cover karta hai, technical root cause team ke samjhane aur recurrence prevent karne ke liye enough detail ke saath, business impact — affected users ki number aur interrupted business operations, applied fix, aur committed preventive actions owners aur timelines ke saath. Selection screen incidents ke liye, preventive actions typically include hain QA test checklist ko update karke missed scenario include karna, transport description standards enforce karna, aur agar ECATT use mein hai toh specific field visibility scenario ko automated tests mein add karna.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A post-incident review covers the complete timeline from when the issue was introduced to when it was resolved, the technical root cause with enough detail for the team to understand and prevent recurrence, the business impact — number of users affected and business operations interrupted, the fix applied, and the preventive actions committed with owners and timelines. For selection screen incidents, preventive actions typically include updating the QA test checklist to include the scenario that was missed, enforcing transport description standards, and adding the specific field visibility scenario to automated tests if ECATT is in use.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: PIR document banaya for company code field incident: Timeline (7 items). Root cause: MODIF ID OR condition introduced by junior developer. Impact: 8 finance users blocked for 45 minutes, 2 urgently needed reports delayed. Fix: Reverted OR condition. Prevention: (1) Peer review mandatory for AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT changes. (2) QA checklist updated with all-fields-visible test scenario. (3) Transport description template updated to require MODIF ID change justification. Prevention items tracked as JIRA tickets.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: PIR blame-focused banana — 'junior developer ne galati ki' pe focus karna. PIR process-focused hona chahiye — 'review process miss kiya, code review mandatory nahi tha' pe focus karo. Blame individual ko demoralize karta hai, process improvement karta hai whole team better.
